protocols.title: FINPROVE MEKi cohort phospho-abs for tCycIF:
steps: FINPROVE MEKi cohort phospho-abs for tCycIF: ORDERED ANTIBODIES: pErk: 4284S (CST) pATF4/CREB2: 9198S (CST) pSAPK/JNK: MA5-15228 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) Issue here is that only pErk is available directly conjugated, and the market of phospho-antibodies is dominated by CST, leading to most antibodies being rabbit monoclonals.

pErk https://www.cellsignal.com/browse?tab=product&search=erk&applications=Immunohistochemistry%20%28Paraffin%29&targetmodifications=phosphate&noscroll=1 AVAILABLE DIRECTLY CONJUGATED Background: Erk is the kinase activated by MEK, and the key signaling molecule in the classical activation of MAPK pathway.
Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway.
JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.
Target selection rationale For more details on MAPK pathway map: https://www.kegg.jp/pathway/map04010 Chosen targets circled in red.
Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity.
Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.
ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g.

Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.
JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e.
activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling.

Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.

Meeting to combine LGSC in FINPROVE and IMPRESS
Date: 24.4.2026
Participants: Anniina Färkkilä, Katriina Jalkanen, Tanja Juslin, Anni Suoknuuti, Line Bjørge
We will aim to join the LGSC cohorts from FINPROVE and IMPRESS. 
Focus is on LGSC both with and without MEK inhibitor treatments
Scientific questions: 
What transcriptomic & spatial proteomic factors drive heterogeneous responses to MEK inhibitors in LGSC?
Explain why patients with similar MAPK pathway mutations show variable clinical outcomes
Investigate mechanisms of response in patients lacking targetable MAPK pathway alterations
Investigate how targetable mutations affect the tumor biology in LGSC
Numbers so far LSGC
profiled: FINPROVE n=XX, IMPRESS = 
MEK treated: 
To combine datasets, The Helsinki team will merge perform NanoString gene expression and CyCIF analyses using a unified pipeline.
Genomics has been done with different platforms. This will not be the primary focus initially (to revisit later).
Both teams need to align definitions of good vs poor response across cohorts before analyses
Additional real-world patients as “Control” arm: Both teams will gather also patients from real-world data from historical cohorts in Bergen and Helsinki (Oncosys-OVA). Anni will send the clinical data requirements to Line for the ethical approval application.
We need to decide on Genomic profiling need and MEK inhibitor usage
MTA drafting. Katriina will consult the HUS lawyer whether the agreement should be IMPRESS–HUS or IMPRESS–University of Helsinki (Färkkilä Lab).
After that Line and Anniina will initiate the process.
Funding: Anni will apply for the NSGO grant in September. Katriina: FINPROVE Syöpäsäätiö funding can also be used for profiling of the Norwegian samples.

FINPROVE MEKi cohort phospho-abs for tCycIF:
ORDERED ANTIBODIES:
pErk: 4284S (CST)
pATF4/CREB2: 9198S (CST)
pSAPK/JNK: MA5-15228 (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Issue here is that only pErk is available directly conjugated, and the market of phospho-antibodies is dominated by CST, leading to most antibodies being rabbit monoclonals.
pErk
https://www.cellsignal.com/browse?tab=product&search=erk&applications=Immunohistochemistry%20%28Paraffin%29&targetmodifications=phosphate&noscroll=1
AVAILABLE DIRECTLY CONJUGATED
Background: Erk is the kinase activated by MEK, and the key signaling molecule in the classical activation of MAPK pathway. Leads to transcription factor activation
pATF4/CREB2
https://www.cellsignal.com/browse?tab=product&search=atf4&categories=Primary%20Antibodies&applications=Immunohistochemistry%20%28Paraffin%29&targetmodifications=phosphate&noscroll=1
Background: Directly downstream of Erk, CREB is a transcription factor that sums various signals and results in activation of numerous downstream pathways. Highly expressed in ovarian cancer
pSAPK/JNK
https://www.cellsignal.com/browse?tab=product&search=jnk&applications=Immunohistochemistry%20%28Paraffin%29&targetmodifications=phosphate&noscroll=1
https://www.thermofisher.com/antibody/product/Phospho-JNK1-JNK2-Thr183-Tyr185-Antibody-clone-E-665-10-Monoclonal/MA5-15228
Background: Important mediator of stress-related signaling. Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.
Target selection rationale

vector_chunks.source_file: 2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text:
itional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.
Target selection rationale

For more details on MAPK pathway map: https://www.kegg.jp/pathway/map04010 
Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.
CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.
JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e. activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling. Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.
Additional issue is whether we can use panCK and PAX8 as cancer markers for the whole cohort. We have one endometrial stromal sarcoma, and one vaginal ADC. 
After confirming with Anni V, the vaginal ADC expresses CK7 -> panCK works for the ADC
The endometrial stromal sarcoma requires an additional marker. 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7528441/ contains info on potential markers.

p44Stitching 
Kuvat powerpointissa 
Muuta huomioitavaa 
_p2: adenocarcinoma vagina 
• Eka vs vika sykli muutamia irtonaisia soluja 
• Heilahtanut jossain kohdassa 
• Taustaa oikea ala kulma & markkerit napannut tähän voimakkaasti -> tod. näk. verisoluja ja verenvuotoa -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spots vähän etenkin kanava 0 DAPI -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasen ala kulma vasta-aine värjäys levinnyt reunoille & pakkautunut (?) -> ei. oikeita signaaleja? 
• Ala vasen tumor cells tuntuu ilmentävän itsessään immuunisolujen markkereita -> ei näytä immune infiltrationilta koska on tumor cell tumia 
• PAX 8 ei toiminut -> cell phenotyping hyvin vaikeaa 
_p5: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat kohdillaan ok 
• Muutamia spotteja joissa yskittäiset solut ei ekassa ja vikassa DAPI syklissä kohdillaan 
• Blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK ja pCREB värjäytyy lähinnä oikean puoleisissa tuumorisoluissa, ei ylä vasemmalla 
• pSTAT valotus ongelma 
_p11: HGSC uterus 
• Ei ihan kohdillaan mutta ok 
• Sykleissä ehkä taitteita -> poista QC:ssä -> käytä segmentointiin ehkä DAPI 3 sykli mielummin? 
• Ei oikeestaan blurry spots
• DAPI 2 syklissä valotus ongelma 
• pCREB ja pERK ei aktiivisia tuumori soluissa? 
• Ki67 kuitenkin super paljon tuumori soluissa? 
• PSTAT valotus ongelma 
• Cyclin D löytyy signaalia, mutta outo spotti keskellä vasemmalla 
_p15: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ei täysin kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots aika paljon etenkin alaosassa kuvaa 
• Taitteita ylä ja alaosassa jonkin verran -> kaikki tulee poistaa 
_p21: HGSC ovary 
• Kohdillaan reunoilta ok hyvin 
• Oikealla blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• DNA vähän blurry -> vaikea analysoida 
• Ei ole käytännössä ollenkaan tuumorisoluja eikä stroomasoluja -> vaikea analysoida mitään? 
• Super paljon CD138, CD31 ja CD163 
_p27: LGSC ovary

• Ei ihan kohdillaan reunoilta mutta ok

• Suurin osa blurry spoteista on tuumori solujen kohdalla -> voi olla vaikea analysoida, poista QC:ssä?

• Vasemmalta ylhäältä puuttuu soluja ainakin vikasta syklistä 
• Ylhäällä mahdollisestii verta -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasemmalla outo kohta, ei verta eikä nekroosia -> markkerit rakeisia tässä -> poista QC:ssä?

• Vas ylhäällä epätasainen spotti 
• Melko paljon blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä (eka vs vika sykli DAPI) • pERK not too much in the panCK cells

• Ainakin vikassa DAPIssa puuttuu suuri määrä soluja, näytteeseen on osuttu? -> optimize in segmentation? 
• Vähän blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK more on the edge of the tumor cells mostly

• pERK on the edge of the tumor cells mostly

• Reunoilta ihan ok kohdillaan

Project overview
Nanostring panel & tCycIF imaging on patients with advanced gynecological malignancies in the FINPROVE clinical trial. Special focus on patients treated with MEK inhibitors & LGSC patients. Our aim is to discover correlations between transcriptional signatures, spatial protein expression and treatment responses.

Background
FINPROVE is Finland’s first multi-center, non-randomized phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA-approved targeted therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who have exhausted standard treatments. Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper molecular insight.
Methods
Between 1/2022 and 4/2025, tumors of 765 patients underwent targeted next-generation sequencing (NGS), of which 103 (13%) were gynecological cancers. NGS data were analyzed to identify potentially actionable alterations. A subset of tumors underwent further characterization using NanoString gene expression profiling and single-cell spatial proteomics. To date, 17 samples have been analyzed with tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (tCycIF) and 12 with NanoString nCounter profiling, aiming for a cohort of at least 36 patients.
Results
The most frequent gynecological cancers were high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC, n=28), low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (LGSC, n=14) and uterine leiomyosarcoma (n=9). The most frequent targetable alterations were in KRAS (n=18) and BRAF (n=3). At data cutoff, 28 patients had received targeted treatment with a median treatment duration of 4 months. Among them, 1 complete response, 9 partial responses, 8 stable diseases, and 10 progressions were observed resulting in a disease control rate of 64% at 16 weeks, the trial’s primary endpoint. Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses.

Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses. Moreover, expression of interferon-related genes correlated with response duration. The tCycIF analyses revealed distinct spatial MAPK pathway activation patterns across histologies, with a prominent activation on the tumor boundaries in LGSC.
Conclusions
Frequent KRAS and BRAF alterations were identified in gynecological cancers, with a subset of patients showing clinical benefit from MEK inhibitors correlating with MAPK and interferon-related gene expressions. Spatial analysis revealed MAPK pathway activation localized to tumor boundaries, particularly in LGSC, underscoring the influence of the tumor microenvironment on treatment response.

Project overview
Nanostring panel & tCycIF imaging on patients with advanced gynecological malignancies in the FINPROVE clinical trial. Special focus on patients treated with MEK inhibitors & LGSC patients. Our aim is to discover correlations between transcriptional signatures, spatial protein expression and treatment responses.
